---
title: "BEDC Operation Space-Time"
description: "Interactive Rule 110 space-time diagrams for concrete BEDC operations."
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
---

<style>
.bedc-space-section {
  background: #f7f5ef;
  border-top: 1px solid #ded8ca;
  padding: 2rem 1.2rem 2.5rem;
}
.bedc-space-inner {
  max-width: 1180px;
  margin: 0 auto;
}
.bedc-space-kicker {
  color: #6d675b;
  font-size: 0.76rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  letter-spacing: 0.08em;
  margin-bottom: 0.45rem;
  text-transform: uppercase;
}
.bedc-space-title {
  color: #1f1f1d;
  font-size: 1.6rem;
  line-height: 1.2;
  margin: 0;
}
.bedc-space-lede,
.bedc-space-note {
  color: #3f3d38;
  line-height: 1.6;
  max-width: 880px;
}
.bedc-space-lede {
  margin: 0.7rem 0 0.8rem;
}
.bedc-space-note {
  margin: 1rem 0 0;
}
.bedc-app {
  margin-top: 1.25rem;
}
.bedc-controls {
  align-items: end;
  display: grid;
  gap: 0.8rem;
  grid-template-columns: minmax(220px, 1.2fr) auto minmax(220px, 1fr) minmax(180px, 0.7fr);
  margin-bottom: 0.9rem;
}
.bedc-control {
  display: grid;
  gap: 0.3rem;
}
.bedc-control label,
.bedc-step-readout {
  color: #4d4942;
  font-size: 0.82rem;
  font-weight: 700;
}
.bedc-control select,
.bedc-control input[type="range"],
.bedc-play-button {
  width: 100%;
}
.bedc-control select {
  background: #fffdf8;
  border: 1px solid #c9c2b3;
  border-radius: 6px;
  color: #222;
  font: inherit;
  min-height: 2.35rem;
  padding: 0.35rem 0.55rem;
}
.bedc-play-button {
  background: #222;
  border: 1px solid #222;
  border-radius: 6px;
  color: #fff;
  cursor: pointer;
  font: inherit;
  min-height: 2.35rem;
  padding: 0 0.85rem;
}
.bedc-canvas-shell {
  background: #fffdf8;
  border: 1px solid #c9c2b3;
  border-radius: 6px;
  overflow: auto;
  padding: 0.75rem;
}
.bedc-canvas-shell canvas {
  display: block;
  image-rendering: pixelated;
  max-width: none;
}
.bedc-meta {
  display: grid;
  gap: 0.35rem;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(5, minmax(0, 1fr));
  margin: 0.75rem 0;
}
.bedc-meta-row {
  background: #fffdf8;
  border: 1px solid #d8d0c0;
  border-radius: 6px;
  display: grid;
  gap: 0.18rem;
  min-width: 0;
  padding: 0.55rem 0.65rem;
}
.bedc-meta-row span {
  color: #6d675b;
  font-size: 0.72rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  text-transform: uppercase;
}
.bedc-meta-row code {
  color: #222;
  font-size: 0.8rem;
  overflow-wrap: anywhere;
  white-space: normal;
}
.bedc-legend {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 0.45rem 0.7rem;
}
.bedc-legend-item {
  align-items: center;
  color: #3f3d38;
  display: inline-flex;
  font-size: 0.82rem;
  gap: 0.3rem;
}
.bedc-legend-swatch {
  border: 1px solid rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2);
  display: inline-block;
  height: 0.75rem;
  width: 0.75rem;
}
@media (max-width: 900px) {
  .bedc-controls,
  .bedc-meta {
    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  }
}
</style>

::: {.bedc-space-section}
::: {.bedc-space-inner}
<div class="bedc-space-kicker">Rule 110 substrate</div>
<h2 class="bedc-space-title">BEDC operations as executable space-time diagrams</h2>

Cook 2004 and Martinez 2007 already give the standard Rule 110 ether, glider, and collision pictures. This page is aimed at a different object: concrete BEDC manifest cases such as `msame_refl`, `hsame_refl`, `ext_step`, and `cont_basic`, loaded into the Rule 110 substrate and evolved in the browser.

The black-white field is the cellular automaton itself. The colored overlays are BEDC semantic regions: boundary padding, encoded input payload, event segments, and the decode window whose manifest verdict is shown below the diagram.

```{=html}
<div id="bedc-spacetime-app" class="bedc-app">
  <div class="bedc-controls" aria-label="BEDC Rule 110 controls">
    <div class="bedc-control">
      <label for="bedc-operation-select">Operation</label>
      <select id="bedc-operation-select"></select>
    </div>
    <div class="bedc-control">
      <label>&nbsp;</label>
      <button id="bedc-play-toggle" class="bedc-play-button" type="button">Play</button>
    </div>
    <div class="bedc-control">
      <label for="bedc-step-scrubber">Step <span id="bedc-step-label" class="bedc-step-readout">0 / 0</span></label>
      <input id="bedc-step-scrubber" type="range" min="0" max="0" step="1" value="0">
    </div>
    <div class="bedc-control">
      <label for="bedc-speed-slider">Speed <span id="bedc-speed-label" class="bedc-step-readout">12 fps</span></label>
      <input id="bedc-speed-slider" type="range" min="1" max="30" step="1" value="12">
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="bedc-canvas-shell">
    <canvas id="bedc-spacetime-canvas" width="960" height="420" aria-label="Rule 110 space-time diagram"></canvas>
  </div>
  <div id="bedc-operation-meta" class="bedc-meta"></div>
  <div id="bedc-layer-legend" class="bedc-legend"></div>
</div>
<script src="assets/rule110_evolve.js"></script>
<script src="assets/bedc_spacetime.js"></script>
```

For the reflexive mark and history cases, the two encoded events occupy adjacent payload windows and the manifest checks equality. For `Ext Empty b0 (e0 Empty)`, the overlay separates source history, mark, and result history. For `Cont Empty Empty Empty`, the three history events show the append claim at the level where the direct carrier manifest is decoded.

:::
:::
